name: runpod-eta-estimate
whenToUse: Estimate how long a RunPod SFT run will take (wall-clock) so you know how long to wait, and surface the ETA + expected finish time on the GitHub Actions run page. Accounts for model size, seed count, epochs, train rows, and execution mode.
workflow: Gather inputs: model size (B params), number of seeds, epochs, train-row count (count the JSONL, or pass --train-data), and execution mode (separate-pods / on-pod-parallel / on-pod-sequential / all-seeds-sequential / single).
Run: python3 tools/estimate_runpod_eta.py --model-params 7 --epochs E --train-data <pack> --seeds S --mode M [--no-eval] --markdown
Read the wall-clock point estimate + low/high range; for train-only runs pass --no-eval (on-pod modes already skip eval).

To show it on GitHub: the launcher workflows append this markdown to $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY, so the ETA + expected-finish renders on the Actions run's Summary page. For an ad-hoc estimate, run the tool locally / in any job and echo to $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY.
Report the range and the expected-finish timestamp; treat it as a heuristic (GPU provisioning dominates variance), not a guarantee.
verification: Estimate is a RANGE (low < point < high), not a single number — provisioning/capacity variance is real.
on-pod modes report 0 eval time; separate-pods/single/all-seeds include eval unless --no-eval.
Mode ordering holds: on-pod-parallel < on-pod-sequential < all-seeds-sequential for N>1 seeds.
Calibrated on observed runs (single 7B SFT ~27 min); a fresh run that lands far outside the band should recalibrate TRAIN_RATE_MIN/PROVISION_MIN.
ETA is labelled a heuristic, never reported as a guaranteed completion time.
commonFailures: Forgetting --no-eval for train-only (math-code) runs, overstating the ETA.
Counting rows wrong (e.g. blank lines) so train time is off.
Treating the point estimate as exact when GPU stockout/retries can push past the high bound.
Estimating on-pod-parallel without a multi-GPU pod actually available (then it degrades to sequential).

examples.output: ~22m (range 15-39m); expected finish stamped; on-pod eval skipped.
